reason: The statement applies because the text illustrates the consequences of Saul's disobedience to the specific commandment of the LORD. Saul chose to spare some of the spoils of war, contrary to the explicit instruction to utterly destroy everything. This act of disobedience is highlighted as a rejection of the word of the LORD, leading to his ultimate rejection as king. The emphasis is on the importance of obeying the divine commandments rather than offering sacrifices or burnt offerings. The narrative underscores the significance of personal moral righteousness in following God's commands.
